title: S07E09

jokes.text: This is the Lady Gaga of scavenger hunts. A complete work of genius wrapped up in a disorienting mix of confusing cultural red herrings.
jokes.setup: Ben is trying to describe his elaborate scavenger hunt
jokes.punchline: He compares it to Lady Gaga with unnecessarily verbose description
jokes.explanation: Good character integration - Ben's overly elaborate approach to everything - but slightly wordy

jokes.text: Ron immediately solves the puzzle with complex cryptex and columnar transposition explanation while Ben stands shocked
jokes.setup: Ben created an impossibly complex scavenger hunt to challenge Ron
jokes.punchline: Ron solves it instantly with technical jargon that makes no sense to normal people
jokes.explanation: Perfect character integration - Ron's mysterious competence vs Ben's over-planning. The technical babble is absurd but delivered deadpan.

jokes.text: Keep it. And don't feel bad. It's my curse.
jokes.setup: Ben is devastated his elaborate puzzle was solved instantly
jokes.punchline: Ron treats his puzzle-solving ability like a tragic burden
jokes.explanation: Classic Ron - treating competence as suffering. Great economy and character voice.

jokes.text: Ooh, shop talk. One of my five favorite types of talk, along with pillow, girl, real, and TED.
jokes.setup: Jen mentions 'shop talk' about campaign planning
jokes.punchline: Leslie categorizes types of talk in her systematic way, ending with TED talks
jokes.explanation: Very Leslie - categorizing everything systematically. Good structure building to TED.

jokes.text: Oh, because every surface area in your house is sticky. Last time I was here, I found a melted crayon on my blazer.
jokes.setup: Why is Jen wearing a poncho in the house
jokes.punchline: The Knope household is a disaster zone of kid mess
jokes.explanation: Relatable parent humor with specific detail of melted crayon

jokes.text: I love watching those squashes fly through the air. I kind of like the way they land. Me, too.
jokes.setup: They have to skip the ceremonial squash toss event
jokes.punchline: Both Leslie and Ben are weirdly into watching squash projectiles
jokes.explanation: Sweet moment showing Leslie and Ben's shared weird interests

jokes.text: I already made my pump up mix. There's a surprising amount of Pat Benatar.
jokes.setup: Leslie asks if Ben is ready for intense campaigning
jokes.punchline: Ben's pump-up music is unexpectedly Pat Benatar-heavy
jokes.explanation: Good character detail - Ben's unexpectedly specific music taste

jokes.text: I was talking to Leslie. Me, Leslie? Yeah.
jokes.setup: Jen asks if Ben is ready for campaigning
jokes.punchline: Ben thinks she's talking to him, creating awkward clarification
jokes.explanation: Minor awkward beat, functional but not exceptional

jokes.text: If you were just a ding-dong, I would just slap a flag pin on you, and pour some Valium down your throat. Everything would be fine. It's the smarties that freak people out.
jokes.setup: Explaining the challenge of Leslie being a smart candidate's wife
jokes.punchline: Casually describing drugging a spouse as campaign strategy
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about political consultancy cynicism, well-delivered

jokes.text: You know, why don't you think of it like sex freshman year of college? You just want to get through it as fast as possible without embarrassing yourself.
jokes.setup: Explaining how to handle being a candidate's wife
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to awkward freshman college sex
jokes.explanation: Solid analogy humor with relatable awkwardness

jokes.text: I'm using that as an analogy to help you out because I'm assuming you were a late bloomer. Not me. I crushed it from day one.
jokes.setup: Just made college sex analogy
jokes.punchline: Unnecessarily overshares about her own college sexual prowess
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the TMI moment, fits Jen's inappropriate consultant character

jokes.text: Stephen, be careful! Poncho!
jokes.setup: Established that she wears a poncho due to sticky house
jokes.punchline: She yells 'Poncho!' as warning when kid approaches
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to the poncho setup, good economy

jokes.text: Well, I had hoped you'd choose a more dignified trade like masonry or logging. But you are out of local government, and that's a step in the right direction.
jokes.setup: April got a job with a foundation
jokes.punchline: Ron considers manual labor more dignified than foundation work, but anything is better than government
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron - anti-government stance and blue-collar values perfectly integrated

jokes.text: If you intend to move, I suppose you should return the spare key to my house. The one I gave you years ago when you were my assistant, for emergencies.
jokes.setup: April is moving away from Pawnee
jokes.punchline: Ron's emotional response is bureaucratic key return request
jokes.explanation: Classic Ron emotional avoidance - expressing care through practical matters

jokes.text: Last contest's loser was all women.
jokes.setup: Describing the Pie Mary contest tradition
jokes.punchline: Casually notes the inherent sexism of the entire concept
jokes.explanation: Brilliant economy - perfectly captures the absurdity of gendered political traditions

jokes.text: Last contest's loser was all women.
jokes.setup: Describes the Pie Mary contest winner
jokes.punchline: Points out the inherent sexism of the competition
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about gender dynamics in political traditions

jokes.text: Leslie, you made it pretty clear that you don't think homemaking is important. Do you ever cook for your children? And who's even watching your children right now while you're out God only knows where?
jokes.setup: Reporter asking pointed questions about Leslie's priorities
jokes.punchline: Asks who's watching her kids while she's literally standing right in front of him at a public event
jokes.explanation: Good satire of sexist media questioning, though not subtle

jokes.text: Mike Patterson's aggressive questioning about Leslie's parenting and whereabouts
jokes.setup: Reporter asks about Leslie skipping the pie contest
jokes.punchline: Escalates to absurd personal attacks about her children
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing absurd media sexism, but runs a bit long.

jokes.text: What are you talking about? I'm standing right here in front of you.
jokes.setup: Reporter asks where her kids are while she's 'out God knows where'
jokes.punchline: Leslie points out the obvious - she's at a scheduled public event
jokes.explanation: Solid reaction but predictable response to the setup

jokes.text: Okay, take it easy. Let's not get emotional.
jokes.setup: Leslie gives reasonable response to his unreasonable question
jokes.punchline: He immediately gaslights her as 'emotional' for being logical
jokes.explanation: Good satire of sexist media tropes, though heavy-handed

jokes.text: What other traditions are you against, Leslie? Baseball? Hugging your children? How much did that haircut cost? None of your business. And thank you for noticing.
jokes.setup: Reporter getting more absurd with his accusations
jokes.punchline: Leslie deflects haircut cost question but can't help being pleased someone noticed
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie moment - can't resist being flattered even during hostile questioning

jokes.text: Hey! Yeah. No. No key. Just a note and a movie ticket stub and four teeth.
jokes.setup: Looking for Ron's key in April's hiding spot
jokes.punchline: Finds increasingly weird items including human teeth
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and fits April's creepy aesthetic

jokes.text: If you are looking for Ron's key, I moved it, and you'll never find it. Cordially, April.
jokes.setup: Note left by April about the key
jokes.punchline: Threatening message signed 'Cordially' in classic April style
jokes.explanation: Perfect April - hostile content with formal politeness. Great character voice.

jokes.text: All we gotta do is train Champion to sniff out Ron's key. Okay. All we need is Ron's key to give him a scent. So give me the key and I'll get you that key.
jokes.setup: Trying to solve the missing key problem
jokes.punchline: Andy's circular logic requires the key to find the key
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy logic - well-meaning but completely backwards thinking

jokes.text: Mike, we all know what Ben Wyatt's wife is really saying. By skipping the Pie Mary, she's saying, 'Women who love their families are stupid.'
jokes.setup: TV interview about Leslie skipping pie contest
jokes.punchline: Opponent's wife makes the most uncharitable interpretation possible
jokes.explanation: Good political satire but fairly predictable attack

jokes.text: Oh, I'm just speculating here, but do you think that Leslie Knope's actions indicate that she actually hates her family?
jokes.setup: Building on June's criticism
jokes.punchline: Escalates to absurd speculation about family hatred
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation of media absurdity but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Well, it only took three hours for Jen to be right.
jokes.setup: Jen predicted Leslie would become a distraction
jokes.punchline: Ben acknowledges how quickly the prediction came true
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier warning, nice economy

jokes.text: I can promote my self-published women's rights barbecue cookbook The Feminine Mesquite.
jokes.setup: Leslie will win the pie contest
jokes.punchline: She has a weirdly specific feminist BBQ cookbook to promote
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie - combines feminism with oddly specific interests. Great title construction.

jokes.text: Except you're gonna need to get a new calculator 'cause I crushed this one in a blind rage while I was watching the show.
jokes.setup: Everything's fine with the campaign
jokes.punchline: Leslie destroyed office equipment in anger
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie moment - trying to stay positive while revealing her real feelings

jokes.text: I found this one, but it has a falcon on the top. I'll keep looking. No, that's it! That's it, Andy! Eagle, falcon, whatever. Not whatever. Eagles are more robust, have stronger talents. Didn't go to Bird School much?
jokes.setup: Andy finds wrong bird box, April corrects his eagle/falcon confusion
jokes.punchline: April sarcastically asks if he went to 'Bird School'
jokes.explanation: Good April snark with absurd 'Bird School' concept

jokes.text: I know. And I fished it out, so I can put it on my voodoo doll.
jokes.setup: Garry lost wedding ring down sewer years ago
jokes.punchline: April retrieved it for her voodoo doll of Garry
jokes.explanation: Perfect April - casually revealing deeply creepy behavior. Excellent character integration and dark humor.

jokes.text: We could easily go that route. Initiate Ghost Protocol. We switch faces, we burn our fingertips off with acid, we live in the tunnels beneath the Kremlin. We could do that easily.
jokes.setup: April wants to run away from the lost key problem
jokes.punchline: Andy has elaborate spy movie escape plan ready
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy - completely over-the-top solution with movie references

jokes.text: Garry, I take everything back. I hate you.
jokes.setup: April just said goodbye nicely to Garry
jokes.punchline: Immediately reverses to her normal cruel attitude
jokes.explanation: Perfect April - brief moment of vulnerability immediately covered by cruelty. Great economy.

jokes.text: These appear to be human teeth. And what is this? New Moon? It's a movie, but I've never seen it. It's, like, about lame werewolves and vampires and stuff.
jokes.setup: Ron examining clues from April's box
jokes.punchline: Ron's dismissive description of Twilight movies
jokes.explanation: Good Ron moment - dismissing pop culture, though not particularly original

jokes.text: Dollars to donuts this points us her way. Finally, a scavenger hunt worthy of my time.
jokes.setup: Following April's clue trail
jokes.punchline: Ron excited about April's puzzle after solving Ben's instantly
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to opening puzzle scene, shows Ron's respect for April

jokes.text: Okay, snacks given, kids tucked in, books read, three simultaneous meltdowns for no reason, kids tucked back in, one of them briefly got outside somehow, kids back in bed, they're all napping.
jokes.setup: Ben reporting on putting kids to bed
jokes.punchline: Increasingly chaotic parenting challenges culminating in mysterious escape
jokes.explanation: Great escalation structure, relatable parenting chaos with good rhythm

jokes.text: I even got an extra whipped cream can, one for baking, one for directly into mouth. You know me so well.
jokes.setup: Ann brought baking supplies
jokes.punchline: Ann knows Leslie will eat whipped cream straight from can
jokes.explanation: Good friendship moment showing Ann knows Leslie's habits

jokes.text: Hope you like pies... In your face. Metaphorically. We'll be civil.
jokes.setup: Threatening to protest Leslie at pie event
jokes.punchline: Accidentally makes pie-in-face pun, then clarifies it's metaphorical
jokes.explanation: Good accidental pun with awkward clarification

jokes.text: Babe, the oven's ready. Chop-chop, time to get bakin'. Daddy want pie.
jokes.setup: Serious conversation about gender politics
jokes.punchline: Ben interrupts with caveman-like pie demand
jokes.explanation: Good timing - undermines serious moment with silly domestic interruption

jokes.text: You kept yelling 'Team Voldemort!' At the screen and we were kicked out. I was furious. You only get to see the second Twilight film for the seventh time once.
jokes.setup: April was on painkillers at Twilight movie
jokes.punchline: Donna's upset about missing her seventh viewing due to April's outburst
jokes.explanation: Perfect Donna - obsessed with Twilight and treating seventh viewing as special occasion

jokes.text: Oh! Babe. You had a crush on me. That's embarrassing. We're married. Still.
jokes.setup: Learning April had crush on Andy years ago
jokes.punchline: Andy thinks it's embarrassing despite them being married
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy - completely backwards logic about marriage and crushes

jokes.text: Garry drops everything down the sewer grate one by one - wedding ring, keys, phone
jokes.setup: Garry trying to retrieve wedding ring from sewer
jokes.punchline: Each attempt to help makes things progressively worse
jokes.explanation: Perfect Garry physical comedy - escalating disasters through his clumsiness

jokes.text: Damn, this takes me back. Mind if I stay and watch? Not at all. I appreciate the company.
jokes.setup: Garry having another disaster
jokes.punchline: Ron nostalgically enjoys watching Garry's suffering
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Ron finding entertainment in Garry's misfortune

jokes.text: Okay, picture this... Crust on the bottom. Filling. Crust on top. What am I describing? A pie. No. But you're in the right 'zone.'
jokes.setup: Ben describing what sounds exactly like a pie
jokes.punchline: It's leading to calzone wordplay with 'zone'
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Ben's calzone obsession, nice wordplay with 'zone'

jokes.text: Pies are just sweet calzones, honey. And I'm good at making calzones.
jokes.setup: Ben trying to solve pie contest problem
jokes.punchline: His solution is his signature calzone obsession
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ben moment - applying his calzone expertise to everything. Great character integration.

jokes.text: Talking 'bout the highway to the calzone zone
jokes.setup: Ben excited about calzone plan
jokes.punchline: Sings Danger Zone parody about calzones
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Ben's calzone obsession with song parody

jokes.text: And she says, 'Hey, Mr. Forgetful.' I mean, those words... They rattle around here for hours.
jokes.setup: Garry describing Gayle's reaction to losing his ring
jokes.punchline: He's deeply wounded by mild nickname 'Mr. Forgetful'
jokes.explanation: Good Garry moment - making gentle teasing sound traumatic

jokes.text: Do what, exactly? Nothing. We're not doing anything. And we definitely do not live down here now.
jokes.setup: Ron asks what they're doing with the rope/choking game
jokes.punchline: Defensive denial reveals they've moved into basement
jokes.explanation: Good awkward revelation about their living situation

jokes.text: I've been there. That's a real tightrope walk.
jokes.setup: Harris describing dangerous choking game
jokes.punchline: Ron casually relates to near-death experience
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron deadpan - treating dangerous activity as casual experience

jokes.text: We're dreaming together, We're sleeping together, But not like that
jokes.setup: They share dreams because they're best friends
jokes.punchline: Song clarifying their sleeping arrangement isn't romantic
jokes.explanation: Mild humor from clarifying their relationship

jokes.text: So we're looking for a racist ghost with black hair who likes shoeshines.
jokes.setup: Trying to decode basement dwellers' ghost story
jokes.punchline: April's practical summary of absurd clues
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan synthesis of ridiculous information

jokes.text: We are very different people.
jokes.setup: Garry describes 'Bozo' as the B-word from Gayle
jokes.punchline: Donna's understated reaction to their different perspectives
jokes.explanation: Perfect understated reaction. Great economy and timing.

jokes.text: Mema was no Leslie Knope. She didn't have a PhD, but she sure knew a lot about P-l-E.
jokes.setup: Talking about grandmother's qualifications
jokes.punchline: Spells out P-I-E for pie expertise pun
jokes.explanation: Weak pun that feels forced and obvious

jokes.text: Now, pack your suitcases, because we're going to a little town Called Napoli... In Italy.
jokes.setup: Ben presenting his calzone at pie contest
jokes.punchline: Introduces calzone with tourist-guide enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Good Ben moment treating calzone presentation like travel show

jokes.text: Men have had a very rough go of it, for... Just recently.
jokes.setup: Explaining men's rights activism
jokes.punchline: Admits their oppression is very recent
jokes.explanation: Good satirical admission about men's rights complaints

jokes.text: We can all agree that no one likes Leslie Knope.
jokes.setup: Talk show discussing the controversy
jokes.punchline: Host states universal dislike as fact
jokes.explanation: Blunt media bias presented as universal truth

jokes.text: Um. Fierce alert. Eat 'em up, honey.
jokes.setup: Marcia making political statement
jokes.punchline: Marshall's inappropriate supportive comment
jokes.explanation: Good cringe from Marshall's awkward support style

jokes.text: What a surprise. The woman is only talking to other women. Can we have one conversation about feminism where men get to be in charge?
jokes.setup: Panel discussion about feminism
jokes.punchline: Complains men aren't in charge of feminism discussion
jokes.explanation: Good satire of tone-deaf male complaints about feminism

jokes.text: I, Brandi Maxxxx, star of 69 Jump Street and Fifty Shaved Old Gays, have your back.
jokes.setup: Brandi supporting Leslie
jokes.punchline: Lists her pornographic film credits matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Good Brandi moment with absurd porn parody titles

jokes.text: Because June Hartwell is a lukewarm bowl of nothing. She started an awareness campaign called 'Bring a Sweater.' She calls it 'goose bump prevention.'
jokes.setup: Explaining why June isn't scrutinized
jokes.punchline: Her campaign is absurdly mild sweater awareness
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurd political campaign concept - perfectly meaningless cause

jokes.text: It's like taking a shot of tequila. You just gotta force it down, suddenly the world feels so much more pleasant. I do three shots every time I have to enter this house.
jokes.setup: Jen comparing political apology to unpleasant medicine
jokes.punchline: Reveals she actually drinks before visiting Leslie's house
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from metaphor to literal confession

jokes.text: It's like taking a shot of tequila. You just gotta force it down, suddenly the world feels so much more pleasant.
jokes.setup: Jen advises Leslie to just apologize
jokes.punchline: Compares political apology to alcohol
jokes.explanation: Good cynical political advice. The alcohol metaphor feels accurate and darkly funny.

jokes.text: It's like taking a shot of tequila. You just gotta force it down, suddenly the world feels so much more pleasant. I do three shots every time I have to enter this house.
jokes.setup: Comparing apologizing to taking tequila
jokes.punchline: Reveals she actually drinks before entering their house
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and callback to the sticky house problem

jokes.text: I just wanted to remember the good old days, when a shoeshine was two bits, and there were no Mexicans in the music business.
jokes.setup: Nostalgic about old times
jokes.punchline: Casual racism mixed with price nostalgia
jokes.explanation: Effective cringe humor from casual old-timer racism

jokes.text: Hi Andy, I hate people, but you are okay. It's weird. Woof, woof. April.
jokes.setup: April's old note to Andy
jokes.punchline: Minimalist love declaration ending with 'woof woof'
jokes.explanation: Perfect April - expressing deep feeling in most minimal, weird way possible. Great economy and character voice.

jokes.text: Get off my boat!
jokes.setup: Andy and April being cute about their past relationship
jokes.punchline: Ron's disgusted reaction to sentiment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron reaction to emotional displays. Great economy and character consistency.

jokes.text: I love how independent my wife is, and because of that, I will not let her speak.
jokes.setup: Ben defending Leslie's independence
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself
jokes.explanation: Sharp irony highlighting the contradiction in his protective stance

jokes.text: I love how independent my wife is, and because of that, I will not let her speak. That came out wrong.
jokes.setup: Ben trying to defend Leslie's independence
jokes.punchline: Accidentally says the opposite of what he means
jokes.explanation: Good ironic miscommunication - Ben's nerves causing verbal fumbles

jokes.text: Second, the Male Men... Where are you? Ah. You're ridiculous, and men's rights is nothing.
jokes.setup: Leslie addressing various protest groups
jokes.punchline: Dismisses entire men's rights movement in one sentence
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie directness and economy in dismissing ridiculous group

jokes.text: It's a stupid question. Stop asking it. Don't ask it.
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining why 'having it all' is a bad question
jokes.punchline: Increasingly emphatic commands to stop asking
jokes.explanation: Leslie's passionate frustration building through repetition

jokes.text: And then, you know, sometimes I don't.
jokes.setup: Asked if she misses her kids while working
jokes.punchline: Honest admission that sometimes she doesn't miss them
jokes.explanation: Brilliant honest parenting moment - saying what parents think but don't admit

jokes.text: By the way, who is taking care of the kids right now? My mom, babe. Everything's fine.
jokes.setup: Leslie making point about double standards
jokes.punchline: Realizes she actually doesn't know, Ben casually answers
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier question about childcare, nice meta moment

jokes.text: Oh. Sorry. I just got here. I had a broken bike chain. My bad.
jokes.setup: Crowd chanting 'Let Ben speak' after he already spoke
jokes.punchline: One guy missed it due to bike trouble
jokes.explanation: Good small absurdist moment - mundane reason for missing speech

jokes.text: I change my house locks every 16 days. That key has been useless since the second Tuesday after I gave it to you.
jokes.setup: April lost Ron's house key
jokes.punchline: Ron's paranoid security makes the key meaningless anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron moment - extreme preparedness making normal concern irrelevant. Great specificity with '16 days' and 'second Tuesday.'

jokes.text: Because this tree reminds me of you. It's strong. And quiet. And always here when you need it. Or whatever.
jokes.setup: Explaining why she hid key by specific tree
jokes.punchline: Genuine sentiment immediately deflected with 'or whatever'
jokes.explanation: Perfect April - expressing deep feeling then immediately covering with dismissal. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: Not the nice things she said about me, the fact that I buried a large amount of gold under that same tree years ago. I have since moved it. Or have I? Or was I lying the whole time? Or was I lying about lying? Or was there never any gold to begin with? Or...
jokes.setup: Ron feeling kinship with April
jokes.punchline: Reveals increasingly complex web of deception about buried gold
jokes.explanation: Great escalating paranoia spiral - very Ron to have multiple layers of deception about everything

jokes.text: Congratulations, Ben Wyatt, you are this year's IOW Woman of the Year. Son of a bitch.
jokes.setup: Ben supported Leslie speaking her mind
jokes.punchline: He wins 'Woman of the Year' award, Ben's frustrated reaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic ending - Ben's genuine support leads to absurd gender-flipped recognition. Good structure and Ben's reaction.

tmdb_overview: Leslie and Ben come under fire for participating in an old Pawnee tradition. April and Ron go on a scavenger hunt. Donna and Garry stroll down memory lane.
insight: Final stretch leans harder on character beats than sharp jokes at 2.4 per minute.
